title: GitHub Review by greptile-apps[bot]
summary: The reviewer wants a new test added for `InjectOsloPolicyConfig` that covers the empty-path case, asserting that the function returns the exact same map reference (not a copy) when `policyFilePath` is empty — matching the documented no-copy contract that the existing test doesn't verify.

code_comments.author: greptile-apps[bot]
code_comments.resolution: Added the suggested test `TestInjectOsloPolicyConfig_emptyPathReturnsOriginalReference` largely as proposed by the reviewer. The only deviation is that `BeIdenticalTo` cannot be used directly on Go maps (they are not comparable with `==`), so the pointer-identity assertion was implemented using `reflect.ValueOf(result).Pointer()` compared to `reflect.ValueOf(config).Pointer()` instead. The mutation-based assertion (`result["mutated"]` propagating to `config`) was kept as suggested. A new `reflect` import was added to support this.

resolution_summary: Added a new test `TestInjectOsloPolicyConfig_emptyPathReturnsOriginalReference` that verifies the documented no-copy contract when `policyFilePath` is empty. The test confirms the returned map is the same reference as the input (via `reflect.ValueOf().Pointer()` comparison) and that mutating the result also mutates the original config.
